name: VIOLIN

meta.form: mirrored density silhouette per category with a median tick
meta.data_required: categories with a DECLARED density curve (grid + densities) and median

see_instead.when: few enough values to show raw

use_when: statistical audiences comparing multimodal shapes that a box hides
the density curve is already computed and declared, with its bandwidth
not_when: general audiences (violins are widely misread: prefer CH-DST-04 or
small samples (a violin smooths noise into false shape)

data_shape.title: action title with a number
data_shape.source: bottom-left always, must state the density bandwidth
data_shape.categories: array of {label, grid: ascending values, density, median}
data_shape.note: densities are DECLARED inputs; OVP never runs a silent KDE

rules: densities are supplied, never computed silently; the source line
global normalization: silhouette heights comparable across rows
median tick spans the silhouette at its position
qa: densities declared with bandwidth in the source
